summary: Added dependency-free get_observability_contract() helper, focused tests, and README consumer guidance. Optional REST/WebSocket/Prometheus surfaces are explicitly unavailable on this branch because their modules are absent.

verification_commands.command: python3 smoke check importing get_observability_contract, json.dumps(contract), and asserting top-level sections

requirements_met.requirement: Expose a stable machine-readable observability contract
requirements_met.evidence: get_observability_contract() returns JSON-compatible metadata with contract_version and plugin metadata. No REST route exists because no API layer is present on this branch.
Contract status_snapshot.fields documents last_tps, avg_tps, peak_tps, output_tokens, updated_at, updated_monotonic, and session_id with types, units, sources, and freshness/session semantics.
Contract api.surfaces.get_tps_stats documents current helper fields and missing-session behavior; websocket section is present with available=false because no WebSocket module exists.
Contract prometheus section is present with available=false because no prometheus_metrics.py exists, and includes label-cardinality guidance.
Existing hook, status snapshot, public API, session, and thread-safety tests passed unchanged; implementation is additive.
Helper returns static dict metadata without session iteration, SQLite, network, timers, background work, or optional imports; test asserts no _SESSIONS mutation.
README Observability Contract section documents helper usage, helper-only availability, versioning, unknown-field tolerance, freshness/session mismatch handling, and cardinality guidance.
tests/test_api.py adds contract JSON, metadata, snapshot field, API metadata, absent optional-surface, and no-state-mutation tests.

blast_radius_covered.notes: Implementation stayed within bead file ownership; no API/WebSocket/Prometheus files existed to edit.
